segments: مَنْ أَحَبَّ لِلَّهِ وَ أَبْغَضَ لِلَّهِ وَ أَعْطَى لِلَّهِ فَهُوَ مِمَّنْ كَمَلَ إِيمَانُهُ .
arabic.text: مَنْ أَحَبَّ لِلَّهِ وَ أَبْغَضَ لِلَّهِ وَ أَعْطَى لِلَّهِ فَهُوَ مِمَّنْ كَمَلَ إِيمَانُهُ .

segments: A number of our companions from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, and Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Khalid and Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, and Sahl Bin Ziyad, altogether, from Ibn Mahboub, from Ali BinRaib, from Abu Ubeyda Al Haza’a,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘The one who loves for the Sake of Allahazwj and hates for the Sake of Allahazwj, so he is from the ones whose Emān is perfect’.
en.hubeali.text: A number of our companions from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, and Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Khalid and Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, and Sahl Bin Ziyad, altogether, from Ibn Mahboub, from Ali BinRaib, from Abu Ubeyda Al Haza’a, (It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘The one who loves for the Sake of Allahazwj and hates for the Sake of Allahazwj, so he is from the ones whose Emān is perfect’.

segments: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa and Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid and Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father and Sahl ibn Ziyad all from ibn Mahbub from Ali ibn Ri’ab from abu ‘Ubaydah al-Hadhdha’ from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) has said, ‘Whoever loves for the sake of Allah, hates for the sake of Allah and gives for the sake of Allah, he is of those whose belief is complete.’”
en.sarwar.text: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa and Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid and Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father and Sahl ibn Ziyad all from ibn Mahbub from Ali ibn Ri’ab from abu ‘Ubaydah al-Hadhdha’ from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) has said, ‘Whoever loves for the sake of Allah, hates for the sake of Allah and gives for the sake of Allah, he is of those whose belief is complete.’”

titles.ar: بَابُ الْحُبِّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ الْبُغْضِ فِي اللَّهِ
titles.en: Chapter 60 – The love regarding Allahazwj and the hatred regarding Allahazwj

segments: مِنْ أَوْثَقِ عُرَى الْإِيمَانِ أَنْ تُحِبَّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ تُبْغِضَ فِي اللَّهِ وَ تُعْطِيَ فِي اللَّهِ وَ تَمْنَعَ فِي اللَّهِ .
arabic.text: مِنْ أَوْثَقِ عُرَى الْإِيمَانِ أَنْ تُحِبَّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ تُبْغِضَ فِي اللَّهِ وَ تُعْطِيَ فِي اللَّهِ وَ تَمْنَعَ فِي اللَّهِ .

segments: Ibn Mahboub, from Malik Bin Atiyya, from Saeed Al A’araj,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘From the strongest bonds of Emān is that you love regarding Allahazwj and hate regarding Allahazwj, and you give regarding Allahazwj and you deprive regarding Allahazwj’.
en.hubeali.text: Ibn Mahboub, from Malik Bin Atiyya, from Saeed Al A’araj, (It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘From the strongest bonds of Emān is that you love regarding Allahazwj and hate regarding Allahazwj, and you give regarding Allahazwj and you deprive regarding Allahazwj’.

segments: 2. Ibn Mahbub has narrated from Malik ibn ‘Atiyah from Sa’id al-A’raj from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “The strongest ring of belief to hold onto is to love for the sake of Allah and hate for the sake of Allah, to give for the sake of Allah and deny for the sake of Allah.”
en.sarwar.text: 2. Ibn Mahbub has narrated from Malik ibn ‘Atiyah from Sa’id al-A’raj from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “The strongest ring of belief to hold onto is to love for the sake of Allah and hate for the sake of Allah, to give for the sake of Allah and deny for the sake of Allah.”

titles.ar: بَابُ الْحُبِّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ الْبُغْضِ فِي اللَّهِ
titles.en: Chapter 60 – The love regarding Allahazwj and the hatred regarding Allahazwj

segments: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وُدُّ الْمُؤْمِنِ لِلْمُؤْمِنِ فِي اللَّهِ مِنْ أَعْظَمِ شُعَبِ الْإِيمَانِ أَلَا وَ مَنْ أَحَبَّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ أَبْغَضَ فِي اللَّهِ وَ أَعْطَى فِي اللَّهِ وَ مَنَعَ فِي اللَّهِ فَهُوَ مِنْ أَصْفِيَاءِ اللَّهِ .
arabic.text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وُدُّ الْمُؤْمِنِ لِلْمُؤْمِنِ فِي اللَّهِ مِنْ أَعْظَمِ شُعَبِ الْإِيمَانِ أَلَا وَ مَنْ أَحَبَّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ أَبْغَضَ فِي اللَّهِ وَ أَعْطَى فِي اللَّهِ وَ مَنَعَ فِي اللَّهِ فَهُوَ مِنْ أَصْفِيَاءِ اللَّهِ .

segments: Ibn Mahboub, from Abu Ja’far Muhammad Bin Al Numan Al Ahowl, Sahib Al Taaq, from Sallam Al Mustaneer,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’farasws having said: ‘Rasool-Allahsaww said: ‘Cordiality of the Believer to the Believer regarding Allahazwj is from the greatest of the branches of Emān. Indeed! And the one who loves regarding Allahazwj and hates regarding Allahazwj, and gives regarding Allahazwj and prevents regarding Allahazwj, so he is from the elites of Allahazwj’.
en.hubeali.text: Ibn Mahboub, from Abu Ja’far Muhammad Bin Al Numan Al Ahowl, Sahib Al Taaq, from Sallam Al Mustaneer, (It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’farasws having said: ‘Rasool-Allahsaww said: ‘Cordiality of the Believer to the Believer regarding Allahazwj is from the greatest of the branches of Emān. Indeed! And the one who loves regarding Allahazwj and hates regarding Allahazwj, and gives regarding Allahazwj and prevents regarding Allahazwj, so he is from the elites of Allahazwj’.

segments: 3. Ibn Mahbub has narrated from abu Ja’far ibn Muhammad al-Nu’man al-Ahwal Sahib al-Taq from Salam ibn al-Mustanir from abu Ja’far (a.s.) who has said the following: “The Messenger of Allah has said, ‘Love of a believer for another believer only for the sake of Allah is of the greatest branches of belief. It is certain that whoever loves for the sake of Allah, hates for the sake of Allah, gives for the sake of Allah and denies for the sake of Allah is of the chosen people of Allah.’”
en.sarwar.text: 3. Ibn Mahbub has narrated from abu Ja’far ibn Muhammad al-Nu’man al-Ahwal Sahib al-Taq from Salam ibn al-Mustanir from abu Ja’far (a.s.) who has said the following: “The Messenger of Allah has said, ‘Love of a believer for another believer only for the sake of Allah is of the greatest branches of belief. It is certain that whoever loves for the sake of Allah, hates for the sake of Allah, gives for the sake of Allah and denies for the sake of Allah is of the chosen people of Allah.’”

titles.ar: بَابُ الْحُبِّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ الْبُغْضِ فِي اللَّهِ
titles.en: Chapter 60 – The love regarding Allahazwj and the hatred regarding Allahazwj

segments: سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ إِنَّ الْمُتَحَابِّينَ فِي اللَّهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ عَلَى مَنَابِرَ مِنْ نُورٍ قَدْ أَضَاءَ نُورُ وُجُوهِهِمْ وَ نُورُ أَجْسَادِهِمْ وَ نُورُ مَنَابِرِهِمْ كُلَّ شَيْ‏ءٍ حَتَّى يُعْرَفُوا بِهِ فَيُقَالُ هَؤُلَاءِ الْمُتَحَابُّونَ فِي اللَّهِ .
arabic.text: سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ إِنَّ الْمُتَحَابِّينَ فِي اللَّهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ عَلَى مَنَابِرَ مِنْ نُورٍ قَدْ أَضَاءَ نُورُ وُجُوهِهِمْ وَ نُورُ أَجْسَادِهِمْ وَ نُورُ مَنَابِرِهِمْ كُلَّ شَيْ‏ءٍ حَتَّى يُعْرَفُوا بِهِ فَيُقَالُ هَؤُلَاءِ الْمُتَحَابُّونَ فِي اللَّهِ .

segments: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moalla Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Al Washa, from Ali Bin Abu Hamza, from Abu Baseer,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I heard him<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘The ones who love regarding Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would be upon Pulpits of light on the Day of Judgement. The light of their faces, and the light of their bodies, and the light of their pulpits would have illuminated everything to the extent that they would be recognised by it, so it would be said, ‘They are the ones who loved regarding Allah<sup>azwj</sup>’.
en.hubeali.text:
Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moalla Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Al Washa, from Ali Bin Abu Hamza, from Abu Baseer,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I heard him<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘The ones who love regarding Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would be upon Pulpits of light on the Day of Judgement. The light of their faces, and the light of their bodies, and the light of their pulpits would have illuminated everything to the extent that they would be recognised by it, so it would be said, ‘They are the ones who loved regarding Allah<sup>azwj</sup>’.

segments: 4. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from Mu’alla ibn Muhammad from al- Hassan ibn Ali al-Washsha’ from Ali ibn abu Hamza from abu Basir from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Abu Basir has said, ‘I heard the Imam saying, “Those who love each other for the sake of Allah, on the Day of Judgment will be seen on the pulpits of light. The light of their faces, the light of their bodies and the light of their pulpits will light up all things so much so that they will become known for it, and they will be called people loving for the sake of Allah.””’’
en.sarwar.text: 4. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from Mu’alla ibn Muhammad from al- Hassan ibn Ali al-Washsha’ from Ali ibn abu Hamza from abu Basir from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Abu Basir has said, ‘I heard the Imam saying, “Those who love each other for the sake of Allah, on the Day of Judgment will be seen on the pulpits of light. The light of their faces, the light of their bodies and the light of their pulpits will light up all things so much so that they will become known for it, and they will be called people loving for the sake of Allah.””’’

titles.ar: بَابُ الْحُبِّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ الْبُغْضِ فِي اللَّهِ
titles.en: Chapter 60 – The love regarding Allahazwj and the hatred regarding Allahazwj

segments: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْحُبِّ وَ الْبُغْضِ أَ مِنَ الْإِيمَانِ هُوَ فَقَالَ وَ هَلِ الْإِيمَانُ إِلَّا الْحُبُّ وَ الْبُغْضُ ثُمَّ تَلَا هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ حَبَّبَ إِلَيْكُمُ الْإِيمانَ وَ زَيَّنَهُ فِي قُلُوبِكُمْ وَ كَرَّهَ إِلَيْكُمُ الْكُفْرَ وَ الْفُسُوقَ وَ الْعِصْيانَ أُولئِكَ هُمُ الرَّاشِدُونَ .
arabic.text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْحُبِّ وَ الْبُغْضِ أَ مِنَ الْإِيمَانِ هُوَ فَقَالَ وَ هَلِ الْإِيمَانُ إِلَّا الْحُبُّ وَ الْبُغْضُ ثُمَّ تَلَا هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ حَبَّبَ إِلَيْكُمُ الْإِيمانَ وَ زَيَّنَهُ فِي قُلُوبِكُمْ وَ كَرَّهَ إِلَيْكُمُ الْكُفْرَ وَ الْفُسُوقَ وَ الْعِصْيانَ أُولئِكَ هُمُ الرَّاشِدُونَ .

segments: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Hammad, from Hareyz, from Fuzayl Bin Yasaar who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullahasws about the love and the hatred, are these from the Emān?’ So heasws said: ‘And is the Emān anything but the love and the hatred?’ Then heasws recited these Verses [49:7] but Allah has endeared the Eman to you and has Adorned it in your hearts, and He has made hateful to you the unbelief and transgression and disobedience; these it is that are the followers of a right way’.
en.hubeali.text: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Hammad, from Hareyz, from Fuzayl Bin Yasaar who said, ‘I asked Abu Abdullahasws about the love and the hatred, are these from the Emān?’ So heasws said: ‘And is the Emān anything but the love and the hatred?’ Then heasws recited these Verses [49:7] but Allah has endeared the Eman to you and has Adorned it in your hearts, and He has made hateful to you the unbelief and transgression and disobedience; these it is that are the followers of a right way’.

segments: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Hammad from Hariz from Fudayl ibnYasar who has said the following: “I asked abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) ‘Are love and hate part of belief?’ The Imam asked, ‘Is belief anything but love and hate?’ He then read the words of Allah, ‘. . . But Allah has endeared belief to you and has made it attractive to your hearts. He has made disbelief, evil deeds and disobedience hateful to you. Such people will have the right guidance.’” [49:7]
en.sarwar.text: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Hammad from Hariz from Fudayl ibnYasar who has said the following: “I asked abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) ‘Are love and hate part of belief?’ The Imam asked, ‘Is belief anything but love and hate?’ He then read the words of Allah, ‘. . . But Allah has endeared belief to you and has made it attractive to your hearts. He has made disbelief, evil deeds and disobedience hateful to you. Such people will have the right guidance.’” [49:7]

titles.ar: بَابُ الْحُبِّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ الْبُغْضِ فِي اللَّهِ
titles.en: Chapter 60 – The love regarding Allahazwj and the hatred regarding Allahazwj

segments: مَا أَعْلَمُ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُدْرِكٍ الطَّائِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) لِأَصْحَابِهِ أَيُّ عُرَى الْإِيمَانِ أَوْثَقُ فَقَالُوا اللَّهُ وَ رَسُولُهُ أَعْلَمُ وَ قَالَ بَعْضُهُمُ الصَّلَاةُ وَ قَالَ بَعْضُهُمُ الزَّكَاةُ وَ قَالَ بَعْضُهُمُ الصِّيَامُ وَ قَالَ بَعْضُهُمُ الْحَجُّ وَ الْعُمْرَةُ وَ قَالَ بَعْضُهُمُ الْجِهَادُ

فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) لِكُلِّ مَا قُلْتُمْ فَضْلٌ وَ لَيْسَ بِهِ وَ لَكِنْ أَوْثَقُ عُرَى الْإِيمَانِ الْحُبُّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ الْبُغْضُ فِي اللَّهِ وَ تَوَالِي أَوْلِيَاءِ اللَّهِ وَ التَّبَرِّي مِنْ أَعْدَاءِ اللَّهِ .
arabic.text:
مَا أَعْلَمُ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُدْرِكٍ الطَّائِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) لِأَصْحَابِهِ أَيُّ عُرَى الْإِيمَانِ أَوْثَقُ فَقَالُوا اللَّهُ وَ رَسُولُهُ أَعْلَمُ وَ قَالَ بَعْضُهُمُ الصَّلَاةُ وَ قَالَ بَعْضُهُمُ الزَّكَاةُ وَ قَالَ بَعْضُهُمُ الصِّيَامُ وَ قَالَ بَعْضُهُمُ الْحَجُّ وَ الْعُمْرَةُ وَ قَالَ بَعْضُهُمُ الْجِهَادُ

فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) لِكُلِّ مَا قُلْتُمْ فَضْلٌ وَ لَيْسَ بِهِ وَ لَكِنْ أَوْثَقُ عُرَى الْإِيمَانِ الْحُبُّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ الْبُغْضُ فِي اللَّهِ وَ تَوَالِي أَوْلِيَاءِ اللَّهِ وَ التَّبَرِّي مِنْ أَعْدَاءِ اللَّهِ .

segments: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Abu Abdullah, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Abu Al Hassan Ali in Yahya among what he knew from Amro Bin Mudrak Al Taiy,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘Rasool-Allahsaww said to hissaww companions: ‘Which bond of the Emān is the strongest?’ So they said, ‘Allahazwj and Hisazwj Rasoolsaww are more knowing’, and some of them said, ‘The Salāt’, and some of them said, ‘The Zakāt’, and some of them said, ‘The Fasts’, and some of them said, ‘The Hajj and the Umrah’, and some of them said, ‘The Jihād’.

So Rasool-Allahsaww said: ‘For everything what you said, there is a merit, and that is not it, but the strongest of the bonds of the Emān is the love regarding Allahazwj, and the hatred regarding Allahazwj, and the Wilayah of the Guardiansasws of Allahazwj, and the disavowing from the enemies of Allahazwj’.
en.hubeali.text: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Abu Abdullah, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Abu Al Hassan Ali in Yahya among what he knew from Amro Bin Mudrak Al Taiy, (It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘Rasool-Allahsaww said to hissaww companions: ‘Which bond of the Emān is the strongest?’ So they said, ‘Allahazwj and Hisazwj Rasoolsaww are more knowing’, and some of them said, ‘The Salāt’, and some of them said, ‘The Zakāt’, and some of them said, ‘The Fasts’, and some of them said, ‘The Hajj and the Umrah’, and some of them said, ‘The Jihād’. So Rasool-Allahsaww said: ‘For everything what you said, there is a merit, and that is not it, but the strongest of the bonds of the Emān is the love regarding Allahazwj, and the hatred regarding Allahazwj, and the Wilayah of the Guardiansasws of Allahazwj, and the disavowing from the enemies of Allahazwj’.

segments: 6. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn abu ‘Abd Allah from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from abu al-Hassan Ali ibn Yahya (as far as I know) from ‘Amr ibn Mudrik al-Ta’i from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “The Messenger of Allah once asked his companions, ‘Which ring of belief is stronger to hold on to?’ They said, ‘Allah and His Messenger know best.’ Certain individuals among them said, ‘It is prayer,’ others said, ‘It is al-Zakat (charity)’, a few of them said, ‘It is fasting’, others said, ‘It is Hajj and ‘Umrah’ and still others said, ‘It is Jihad (hard work against the enemies).’ “The Messenger of Allah then said, ‘For everything you said there is a merit but is not such a ring. In fact the strongest ring of belief to hold on to is love for the sake of Allah, hate for the sake of Allah, support and love those who have received authority from Allah and to disassociate from the enemies of Allah.’”
en.sarwar.text: 6. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn abu ‘Abd Allah from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from abu al-Hassan Ali ibn Yahya (as far as I know) from ‘Amr ibn Mudrik al-Ta’i from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “The Messenger of Allah once asked his companions, ‘Which ring of belief is stronger to hold on to?’ They said, ‘Allah and His Messenger know best.’ Certain individuals among them said, ‘It is prayer,’ others said, ‘It is al-Zakat (charity)’, a few of them said, ‘It is fasting’, others said, ‘It is Hajj and ‘Umrah’ and still others said, ‘It is Jihad (hard work against the enemies).’ “The Messenger of Allah then said, ‘For everything you said there is a merit but is not such a ring. In fact the strongest ring of belief to hold on to is love for the sake of Allah, hate for the sake of Allah, support and love those who have received authority from Allah and to disassociate from the enemies of Allah.’”

titles.ar: بَابُ الْحُبِّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ الْبُغْضِ فِي اللَّهِ
titles.en: Chapter 60 – The love regarding Allahazwj and the hatred regarding Allahazwj

segments: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) الْمُتَحَابُّونَ فِي اللَّهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ عَلَى أَرْضِ زَبَرْجَدَةٍ خَضْرَاءَ فِي ظِلِّ عَرْشِهِ عَنْ يَمِينِهِ وَ كِلْتَا يَدَيْهِ يَمِينٌ وُجُوهُهُمْ أَشَدُّ بَيَاضاً وَ أَضْوَأُ مِنَ الشَّمْسِ الطَّالِعَةِ يَغْبِطُهُمْ بِمَنْزِلَتِهِمْ كُلُّ مَلَكٍ مُقَرَّبٍ وَ كُلُّ نَبِيٍّ مُرْسَلٍ يَقُولُ النَّاسُ مَنْ هَؤُلَاءِ فَيُقَالُ هَؤُلَاءِ الْمُتَحَابُّونَ فِي اللَّهِ .
arabic.text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) الْمُتَحَابُّونَ فِي اللَّهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ عَلَى أَرْضِ زَبَرْجَدَةٍ خَضْرَاءَ فِي ظِلِّ عَرْشِهِ عَنْ يَمِينِهِ وَ كِلْتَا يَدَيْهِ يَمِينٌ وُجُوهُهُمْ أَشَدُّ بَيَاضاً وَ أَضْوَأُ مِنَ الشَّمْسِ الطَّالِعَةِ يَغْبِطُهُمْ بِمَنْزِلَتِهِمْ كُلُّ مَلَكٍ مُقَرَّبٍ وَ كُلُّ نَبِيٍّ مُرْسَلٍ يَقُولُ النَّاسُ مَنْ هَؤُلَاءِ فَيُقَالُ هَؤُلَاءِ الْمُتَحَابُّونَ فِي اللَّهِ .

segments: From him, from Muhammad Bin Ali, from Umar Bin Jabala Al Ahmasy, from Abu Al Jaroud,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> having said: ‘Rasool-Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> said: ‘The ones who love regarding Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>, on the Day of Judgement would be upon a land of green emeralds in the Shade of His<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Throne, by His<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Right Hand, and both of His<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Hands are right. Their faces would be intensely white and more illuminating than the emerging sun. Due to their status, they would be the envy of every Angel of Proximity, and every Mursil Prophet<sup style="font-weight: normal">as</sup>. The people would be saying, ‘Who are they?’ So it would be said, ‘They are the ones who loved regarding Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>’’.
en.hubeali.text:
From him, from Muhammad Bin Ali, from Umar Bin Jabala Al Ahmasy, from Abu Al Jaroud,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> having said: ‘Rasool-Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> said: ‘The ones who love regarding Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>, on the Day of Judgement would be upon a land of green emeralds in the Shade of His<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Throne, by His<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Right Hand, and both of His<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Hands are right. Their faces would be intensely white and more illuminating than the emerging sun. Due to their status, they would be the envy of every Angel of Proximity, and every Mursil Prophet<sup style="font-weight: normal">as</sup>. The people would be saying, ‘Who are they?’ So it would be said, ‘They are the ones who loved regarding Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>’’.

segments: 7. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from Muhammad ibn Ali from ‘Umar ibn Jabalah from al-Ahmasi from abu al-Jarud from abu Ja’far (a.s.) who has said the following: “The Messenger of Allah has said, ‘Those who love each other for the sake of Allah, on the Day of Judgment will be found on a land of green emerald in the shadow of the Throne to His right - His both hands are right - their faces intensely white and brighter than the shining sun. Every angel close to Allah and every prophet who is a messenger also will envy their position. People will ask, “Who are they?” It will be said, “They are those who loved each other for the sake of Allah.’””
en.sarwar.text: 7. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from Muhammad ibn Ali from ‘Umar ibn Jabalah from al-Ahmasi from abu al-Jarud from abu Ja’far (a.s.) who has said the following: “The Messenger of Allah has said, ‘Those who love each other for the sake of Allah, on the Day of Judgment will be found on a land of green emerald in the shadow of the Throne to His right - His both hands are right - their faces intensely white and brighter than the shining sun. Every angel close to Allah and every prophet who is a messenger also will envy their position. People will ask, “Who are they?” It will be said, “They are those who loved each other for the sake of Allah.’””

titles.en: Chapter 60 – The love regarding Allahazwj and the hatred regarding Allahazwj

segments: إِذَا جَمَعَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ الْأَوَّلِينَ وَ الْآخِرِينَ قَامَ مُنَادٍ فَنَادَى يُسْمِعُ النَّاسَ فَيَقُولُ أَيْنَ الْمُتَحَابُّونَ فِي اللَّهِ قَالَ فَيَقُومُ عُنُقٌ مِنَ النَّاسِ فَيُقَالُ لَهُمُ اذْهَبُوا إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ

قَالَ فَتَلَقَّاهُمُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ فَيَقُولُونَ إِلَى أَيْنَ فَيَقُولُونَ إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ قَالَ فَيَقُولُونَ فَأَيُّ ضَرْبٍ أَنْتُمْ مِنَ النَّاسِ فَيَقُولُونَ نَحْنُ الْمُتَحَابُّونَ فِي اللَّهِ قَالَ فَيَقُولُونَ وَ أَيَّ شَيْ‏ءٍ كَانَتْ أَعْمَالُكُمْ قَالُوا كُنَّا نُحِبُّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ نُبْغِضُ فِي اللَّهِ قَالَ فَيَقُولُونَ نِعْمَ أَجْرُ الْعَامِلِينَ .
arabic.text:
إِذَا جَمَعَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ الْأَوَّلِينَ وَ الْآخِرِينَ قَامَ مُنَادٍ فَنَادَى يُسْمِعُ النَّاسَ فَيَقُولُ أَيْنَ الْمُتَحَابُّونَ فِي اللَّهِ قَالَ فَيَقُومُ عُنُقٌ مِنَ النَّاسِ فَيُقَالُ لَهُمُ اذْهَبُوا إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ

قَالَ فَتَلَقَّاهُمُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ فَيَقُولُونَ إِلَى أَيْنَ فَيَقُولُونَ إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ قَالَ فَيَقُولُونَ فَأَيُّ ضَرْبٍ أَنْتُمْ مِنَ النَّاسِ فَيَقُولُونَ نَحْنُ الْمُتَحَابُّونَ فِي اللَّهِ قَالَ فَيَقُولُونَ وَ أَيَّ شَيْ‏ءٍ كَانَتْ أَعْمَالُكُمْ قَالُوا كُنَّا نُحِبُّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ نُبْغِضُ فِي اللَّهِ قَالَ فَيَقُولُونَ نِعْمَ أَجْرُ الْعَامِلِينَ .

segments: From him, from his father, from Al Nazar Bin Suweyd, from Hisham Bin Salim, from Abu Hamza Al Sumaly,

(It has been narrated) from Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘When Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Gathers the former ones and the later ones, a Caller would stand up and Call out, which the people would hear. So he would be saying: ‘Where are those who loved regarding Allah<sup>azwj</sup>?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘So some necks from the people would arise, and it would be said to them: ‘Go to the Paradise without Reckoning!’

He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘So the Angels would meet them and they would be saying: ‘To where?’ So they would be saying, ‘To the Paradise without Reckoning’. So they would be saying: ‘So which group are you from the people?’ So they would be saying, ‘We are the ones who love regarding Allah<sup>azwj</sup>’. So they would be saying: ‘And which thing was your deeds?’ They would say, ‘We used to love regarding Allah<sup>azwj</sup> and hate regarding Allah<sup>azwj</sup>’. So they would be saying: ‘The best is the Recompense of the workers’.
en.hubeali.text:
From him, from his father, from Al Nazar Bin Suweyd, from Hisham Bin Salim, from Abu Hamza Al Sumaly,

(It has been narrated) from Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘When Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Gathers the former ones and the later ones, a Caller would stand up and Call out, which the people would hear. So he would be saying: ‘Where are those who loved regarding Allah<sup>azwj</sup>?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘So some necks from the people would arise, and it would be said to them: ‘Go to the Paradise without Reckoning!’

He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘So the Angels would meet them and they would be saying: ‘To where?’ So they would be saying, ‘To the Paradise without Reckoning’. So they would be saying: ‘So which group are you from the people?’ So they would be saying, ‘We are the ones who love regarding Allah<sup>azwj</sup>’. So they would be saying: ‘And which thing was your deeds?’ They would say, ‘We used to love regarding Allah<sup>azwj</sup> and hate regarding Allah<sup>azwj</sup>’. So they would be saying: ‘The best is the Recompense of the workers’.

segments: 8. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from his father from al- Nadr ibn Suwayd from Hisham ibn Salim from abu Hamza al-Thumali from Ali ibn al-Husayn (a.s.) who has said the following: “When Allah, the Majestic, the Glorious, will gather together all the people of the past and the later generations, a caller whom everyone will hear will call, ‘Where are those whose love was just for the sake of Allah?’ Certain necks will rise from among the people and it will be said to them, ‘Go to paradise without any questions asked.’ The Imam said, ‘The angels will meet them asking, “Where are you going?” They will say, “We are going to paradise without any questions asked.” The Imam said, “The angels will ask, ‘What kind of people are you?’ They will say, ‘We are the people who loved just for the sake of Allah.’ The Imam said, ‘The angels will ask, “What were your deeds?” They will reply, “We loved for the sake of Allah and hated just for the sake of Allah.” The Imam said, ‘The angels will say, “How wonderful is the reward of the doers of good deeds!””’
en.sarwar.text: 8. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from his father from al- Nadr ibn Suwayd from Hisham ibn Salim from abu Hamza al-Thumali from Ali ibn al-Husayn (a.s.) who has said the following: “When Allah, the Majestic, the Glorious, will gather together all the people of the past and the later generations, a caller whom everyone will hear will call, ‘Where are those whose love was just for the sake of Allah?’ Certain necks will rise from among the people and it will be said to them, ‘Go to paradise without any questions asked.’ The Imam said, ‘The angels will meet them asking, “Where are you going?” They will say, “We are going to paradise without any questions asked.” The Imam said, “The angels will ask, ‘What kind of people are you?’ They will say, ‘We are the people who loved just for the sake of Allah.’ The Imam said, ‘The angels will ask, “What were your deeds?” They will reply, “We loved for the sake of Allah and hated just for the sake of Allah.” The Imam said, ‘The angels will say, “How wonderful is the reward of the doers of good deeds!””’

segments: ثَلَاثٌ مِنْ عَلَامَاتِ الْمُؤْمِنِ عِلْمُهُ بِاللَّهِ وَ مَنْ يُحِبُّ وَ مَنْ يُبْغِضُ .
arabic.text: ثَلَاثٌ مِنْ عَلَامَاتِ الْمُؤْمِنِ عِلْمُهُ بِاللَّهِ وَ مَنْ يُحِبُّ وَ مَنْ يُبْغِضُ .

segments: From him, from Ali Bin Hassan, from the one who mentioned it, from Dawood Bin Farqad,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘There (things) are from the signs of a Momin (the Believer) – His knowledge in Allahazwj, and the one whom he loves, and the one whom he hates’.
en.hubeali.text: From him, from Ali Bin Hassan, from the one who mentioned it, from Dawood Bin Farqad, (It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘There (things) are from the signs of a Momin (the Believer) – His knowledge in Allahazwj, and the one whom he loves, and the one whom he hates’.

segments: 9. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from Ali ibn Hassan from those he has mentioned (in his book) from Dawud ibn Farqad from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) has said, ‘Three things are of the signs of a believer: His knowledge of Allah, who to love and who to hate.’”
en.sarwar.text: 9. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from Ali ibn Hassan from those he has mentioned (in his book) from Dawud ibn Farqad from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) has said, ‘Three things are of the signs of a believer: His knowledge of Allah, who to love and who to hate.’”

titles.en: Chapter 60 – The love regarding Allahazwj and the hatred regarding Allahazwj

segments: إِنَّ الرَّجُلَ لَيُحِبُّكُمْ وَ مَا يَعْرِفُ مَا أَنْتُمْ عَلَيْهِ فَيُدْخِلُهُ اللَّهُ الْجَنَّةَ بِحُبِّكُمْ وَ إِنَّ الرَّجُلَ لَيُبْغِضُكُمْ وَ مَا يَعْرِفُ مَا أَنْتُمْ عَلَيْهِ فَيُدْخِلُهُ اللَّهُ بِبُغْضِكُمُ النَّارَ .
arabic.text: إِنَّ الرَّجُلَ لَيُحِبُّكُمْ وَ مَا يَعْرِفُ مَا أَنْتُمْ عَلَيْهِ فَيُدْخِلُهُ اللَّهُ الْجَنَّةَ بِحُبِّكُمْ وَ إِنَّ الرَّجُلَ لَيُبْغِضُكُمْ وَ مَا يَعْرِفُ مَا أَنْتُمْ عَلَيْهِ فَيُدْخِلُهُ اللَّهُ بِبُغْضِكُمُ النَّارَ .

segments: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hisham Bin Salim, and Hafs Bin Al Bakhtary,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The man would love you all and he does not recognise what you are upon (the Wilayah), so Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Enter him into the Paradise due to his love for you; and the man would hate you all and does not recognise what you are upon (the Wilayah), so Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Enter him into the Fire due to his hatred for you’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hisham Bin Salim, and Hafs Bin Al Bakhtary,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The man would love you all and he does not recognise what you are upon (the Wilayah), so Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Enter him into the Paradise due to his love for you; and the man would hate you all and does not recognise what you are upon (the Wilayah), so Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Enter him into the Fire due to his hatred for you’.

segments: 10. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hisham ibn Salim and from Hafs ibn al-Bakhtari from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “The man loves you (followers of Ahl al-Bayt) without knowing your belief and Allah admits him to paradise because he loves you. A man hates you without knowing what your belief is and Allah then admits him in the fire for hating you.”
en.sarwar.text: 10. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hisham ibn Salim and from Hafs ibn al-Bakhtari from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “The man loves you (followers of Ahl al-Bayt) without knowing your belief and Allah admits him to paradise because he loves you. A man hates you without knowing what your belief is and Allah then admits him in the fire for hating you.”

titles.ar: بَابُ الْحُبِّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ الْبُغْضِ فِي اللَّهِ
titles.en: Chapter 60 – The love regarding Allahazwj and the hatred regarding Allahazwj

segments: إِذَا أَرَدْتَ أَنْ تَعْلَمَ أَنَّ فِيكَ خَيْراً فَانْظُرْ إِلَى قَلْبِكَ فَإِنْ كَانَ يُحِبُّ أَهْلَ طَاعَةِ اللَّهِ وَ يُبْغِضُ أَهْلَ مَعْصِيَتِهِ فَفِيكَ خَيْرٌ وَ اللَّهُ يُحِبُّكَ وَ إِنْ كَانَ يُبْغِضُ أَهْلَ طَاعَةِ اللَّهِ وَ يُحِبُّ أَهْلَ مَعْصِيَتِهِ فَلَيْسَ فِيكَ خَيْرٌ وَ اللَّهُ يُبْغِضُكَ وَ الْمَرْءُ مَعَ مَنْ أَحَبَّ .
arabic.text: إِذَا أَرَدْتَ أَنْ تَعْلَمَ أَنَّ فِيكَ خَيْراً فَانْظُرْ إِلَى قَلْبِكَ فَإِنْ كَانَ يُحِبُّ أَهْلَ طَاعَةِ اللَّهِ وَ يُبْغِضُ أَهْلَ مَعْصِيَتِهِ فَفِيكَ خَيْرٌ وَ اللَّهُ يُحِبُّكَ وَ إِنْ كَانَ يُبْغِضُ أَهْلَ طَاعَةِ اللَّهِ وَ يُحِبُّ أَهْلَ مَعْصِيَتِهِ فَلَيْسَ فِيكَ خَيْرٌ وَ اللَّهُ يُبْغِضُكَ وَ الْمَرْءُ مَعَ مَنْ أَحَبَّ .

segments: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Ibn Al Arzamy, from his father, from Jabir Al Ju’fy,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> having said: ‘Whenever you intend to know that there is goodness in you, so look at your heart. So if it loves the people obedient to Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> and hates the people disobedient to Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>, so there is goodness in you and Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Loves you. And if it was so that it hates the people obedient to Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> and loves the people disobedient to Him<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>, so there is no goodnes in you and Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Hates you. And the person would be with the one who he loves’.
en.hubeali.text:
A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Ibn Al Arzamy, from his father, from Jabir Al Ju’fy,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> having said: ‘Whenever you intend to know that there is goodness in you, so look at your heart. So if it loves the people obedient to Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> and hates the people disobedient to Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>, so there is goodness in you and Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Loves you. And if it was so that it hates the people obedient to Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> and loves the people disobedient to Him<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>, so there is no goodnes in you and Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Hates you. And the person would be with the one who he loves’.

segments: 11. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid from ibn al-‘Arzami from his father from Jabir al-Juhfi from abu Ja’far (a.s.) who has said the following: “If you like to know whether there is anything good in you, look into your heart. If it loves people who obey Allah and hates those who disobey Him, then there is good in you and Allah loves you. If it hates the people who obey Allah and loves those who disobey Allah, then there is nothing good in you and Allah hates you. A man is with what he loves.”
en.sarwar.text: 11. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid from ibn al-‘Arzami from his father from Jabir al-Juhfi from abu Ja’far (a.s.) who has said the following: “If you like to know whether there is anything good in you, look into your heart. If it loves people who obey Allah and hates those who disobey Him, then there is good in you and Allah loves you. If it hates the people who obey Allah and loves those who disobey Allah, then there is nothing good in you and Allah hates you. A man is with what he loves.”

titles.ar: بَابُ الْحُبِّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ الْبُغْضِ فِي اللَّهِ
titles.en: Chapter 60 – The love regarding Allahazwj and the hatred regarding Allahazwj

segments: لَوْ أَنَّ رَجُلًا أَحَبَّ رَجُلًا لِلَّهِ لَأَثَابَهُ اللَّهُ عَلَى حُبِّهِ إِيَّاهُ وَ إِنْ كَانَ الْمَحْبُوبُ فِي عِلْمِ اللَّهِ مِنْ أَهْلِ النَّارِ وَ لَوْ أَنَّ رَجُلًا أَبْغَضَ رَجُلًا لِلَّهِ لَأَثَابَهُ اللَّهُ عَلَى بُغْضِهِ إِيَّاهُ وَ إِنْ كَانَ الْمُبْغَضُ فِي عِلْمِ اللَّهِ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْجَنَّةِ .
arabic.text: لَوْ أَنَّ رَجُلًا أَحَبَّ رَجُلًا لِلَّهِ لَأَثَابَهُ اللَّهُ عَلَى حُبِّهِ إِيَّاهُ وَ إِنْ كَانَ الْمَحْبُوبُ فِي عِلْمِ اللَّهِ مِنْ أَهْلِ النَّارِ وَ لَوْ أَنَّ رَجُلًا أَبْغَضَ رَجُلًا لِلَّهِ لَأَثَابَهُ اللَّهُ عَلَى بُغْضِهِ إِيَّاهُ وَ إِنْ كَانَ الْمُبْغَضُ فِي عِلْمِ اللَّهِ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْجَنَّةِ .

segments: From him, from Abu Ali Al Wasity, from Al Husayn Bin Aban, from the one who mentioned,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘If a man were to love a man for the Sake of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Reward him upon his love for him, and even if it was so that the beloved was from the people of the Fire in the Knowledge of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>; and if a man were to hate a man for the Sake of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Reward him upon his hatred for him, and even if it was so that the hated one was from the people of the Paradise in the Knowledge of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>’.
en.hubeali.text:
From him, from Abu Ali Al Wasity, from Al Husayn Bin Aban, from the one who mentioned,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘If a man were to love a man for the Sake of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Reward him upon his love for him, and even if it was so that the beloved was from the people of the Fire in the Knowledge of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>; and if a man were to hate a man for the Sake of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Reward him upon his hatred for him, and even if it was so that the hated one was from the people of the Paradise in the Knowledge of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>’.

segments: 12. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from abu Ali al-Wasiti from al-Husayn ibn Aban from those whom he has mentioned (in his book) from abu Ja’far (a.s.) who has said the following: “If a man loves another man just for the sake of Allah, Allah will give him good rewards for his loving him, even if that man is of the people of the fire, in the knowledge of Allah. If a man hates another man just for the sake of Allah, Allah will give him good reward for his hating him, even if that man is of the people of paradise, in the knowledge of Allah.”
en.sarwar.text: 12. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from abu Ali al-Wasiti from al-Husayn ibn Aban from those whom he has mentioned (in his book) from abu Ja’far (a.s.) who has said the following: “If a man loves another man just for the sake of Allah, Allah will give him good rewards for his loving him, even if that man is of the people of the fire, in the knowledge of Allah. If a man hates another man just for the sake of Allah, Allah will give him good reward for his hating him, even if that man is of the people of paradise, in the knowledge of Allah.”

titles.ar: بَابُ الْحُبِّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ الْبُغْضِ فِي اللَّهِ
titles.en: Chapter 60 – The love regarding Allahazwj and the hatred regarding Allahazwj

segments: قَدْ يَكُونُ حُبٌّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ رَسُولِهِ وَ حُبٌّ فِي الدُّنْيَا فَمَا كَانَ فِي اللَّهِ وَ رَسُولِهِ فَثَوَابُهُ عَلَى اللَّهِ وَ مَا كَانَ فِي الدُّنْيَا فَلَيْسَ بِشَيْ‏ءٍ .
arabic.text: قَدْ يَكُونُ حُبٌّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ رَسُولِهِ وَ حُبٌّ فِي الدُّنْيَا فَمَا كَانَ فِي اللَّهِ وَ رَسُولِهِ فَثَوَابُهُ عَلَى اللَّهِ وَ مَا كَانَ فِي الدُّنْيَا فَلَيْسَ بِشَيْ‏ءٍ .

segments: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Al Husayn BiN Saeed, from Al Nazar Bin Suweyd, from Yahya Al Halby, from Bashir Al Kunasy,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘There may happen to be love regarding Allah<sup>azwj</sup> and His<sup>azwj</sup> Rasool<sup>saww</sup> and love regarding the world. So whatever was regarding Allah<sup>azwj</sup> and His<sup>azwj</sup> Rasool<sup>saww</sup>, so its Reward is upon Allah<sup>azwj</sup>; and whatever was regarding the world, so it is not with anything’.
en.hubeali.text:
Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Al Husayn BiN Saeed, from Al Nazar Bin Suweyd, from Yahya Al Halby, from Bashir Al Kunasy,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘There may happen to be love regarding Allah<sup>azwj</sup> and His<sup>azwj</sup> Rasool<sup>saww</sup> and love regarding the world. So whatever was regarding Allah<sup>azwj</sup> and His<sup>azwj</sup> Rasool<sup>saww</sup>, so its Reward is upon Allah<sup>azwj</sup>; and whatever was regarding the world, so it is not with anything’.

segments: 13. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from al-Husayn ibn Sa’id from al-Nadr ibn Suwayd from Yahya al-Halabi from Bashir al-Kannasi from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Sometimes love is for the sake of Allah and the Messenger of Allah and sometimes it is for the sake of worldly matters. For love, which is for the sake of Allah and the Messenger of Allah, the reward is with Allah. For love that is for the sake of worldly matters there is nothing.”
en.sarwar.text: 13. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from al-Husayn ibn Sa’id from al-Nadr ibn Suwayd from Yahya al-Halabi from Bashir al-Kannasi from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Sometimes love is for the sake of Allah and the Messenger of Allah and sometimes it is for the sake of worldly matters. For love, which is for the sake of Allah and the Messenger of Allah, the reward is with Allah. For love that is for the sake of worldly matters there is nothing.”

segments: إِنَّ الْمُسْلِمَيْنِ يَلْتَقِيَانِ فَأَفْضَلُهُمَا أَشَدُّهُمَا حُبّاً لِصَاحِبِهِ .

segments: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Usman Bin Isa, from Sama’at Bin Mihran,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘If two Muslims were to meet, so the superior of the two would be the one with more intense love for his companion’.
en.hubeali.text:
A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Usman Bin Isa, from Sama’at Bin Mihran,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘If two Muslims were to meet, so the superior of the two would be the one with more intense love for his companion’.

segments: 14. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa from Sama’a ibn Mehran from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Of the two Muslims who meet each other, the more meritorious is the one who loves his companion more intensely.”
en.sarwar.text: 14. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa from Sama’a ibn Mehran from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Of the two Muslims who meet each other, the more meritorious is the one who loves his companion more intensely.”

titles.ar: بَابُ الْحُبِّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ الْبُغْضِ فِي اللَّهِ
titles.en: Chapter 60 – The love regarding Allahazwj and the hatred regarding Allahazwj

segments: مَا الْتَقَى مُؤْمِنَانِ قَطُّ إِلَّا كَانَ أَفْضَلُهُمَا أَشَدَّهُمَا حُبّاً لِأَخِيهِ .
arabic.text: مَا الْتَقَى مُؤْمِنَانِ قَطُّ إِلَّا كَانَ أَفْضَلُهُمَا أَشَدَّهُمَا حُبّاً لِأَخِيهِ .

segments: From him, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, and Ibn Fazzal, from Safwan Al Jammal,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘No two ‘Momineen’ (Believers) would meet at all except that the superior of the two would be with more intense love for his brother’.
en.hubeali.text: From him, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, and Ibn Fazzal, from Safwan Al Jammal, (It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘No two ‘Momineen’ (Believers) would meet at all except that the superior of the two would be with more intense love for his brother’.

segments: 15. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from Ahmad ibn abu Nasr and ibn Faddal from Safwan al-Jammal from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Of the two believers who meet each other, the more meritorious one is the one who loves his brethren more intensely.”
en.sarwar.text: 15. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from Ahmad ibn abu Nasr and ibn Faddal from Safwan al-Jammal from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Of the two believers who meet each other, the more meritorious one is the one who loves his brethren more intensely.”

titles.ar: بَابُ الْحُبِّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ الْبُغْضِ فِي اللَّهِ
titles.en: Chapter 60 – The love regarding Allahazwj and the hatred regarding Allahazwj

segments: كُلُّ مَنْ لَمْ يُحِبَّ عَلَى الدِّينِ وَ لَمْ يُبْغِضْ عَلَى الدِّينِ فَلَا دِينَ لَهُ .
arabic.text: كُلُّ مَنْ لَمْ يُحِبَّ عَلَى الدِّينِ وَ لَمْ يُبْغِضْ عَلَى الدِّينِ فَلَا دِينَ لَهُ .

segments: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Imran Al Sabi’e, from Abdullah Bin Jabala, from Is’haq Bin Ammar,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Everyone who does not love upon the Religion and does not hate upon the Religion, so there is no Religion for him’.
en.hubeali.text:
Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Imran Al Sabi’e, from Abdullah Bin Jabala, from Is’haq Bin Ammar,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Everyone who does not love upon the Religion and does not hate upon the Religion, so there is no Religion for him’.

segments: 16. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Imran al-Subay’i from ‘Abd Allah ibn Jabalah from Ishaq ibn ‘Ammar from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Whoever does not love because of religion and does not hate because of religion has no religion.”
en.sarwar.text: 16. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Imran al-Subay’i from ‘Abd Allah ibn Jabalah from Ishaq ibn ‘Ammar from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Whoever does not love because of religion and does not hate because of religion has no religion.”
